Highlights of the Chauffeured Vineyards Adventure include: - VIP guided adventure to stunning and unique local winery estates.- Ample pours of award-winning local wines.- A delicious appetizer platte AND a full meal!- Fun edutainment. Entertaining commentary on local history, and viticulture.- Door-to-door transportation (from most San Diego locations).- Wine tasting appreciation presentation, and guided winery tour. Learn about wine making and wine pairing. WHENDaily, year-round. Rain or shine. HOW MANY POURSAbout a bottle of wine per person (in different sized pours). WHAT FOOD IS INCLUDEDSavor a delicious charcuterie platter (gourmet cheeses, meats, fruits, breads) AND a full meal overlooking the winery's grounds: fresh garden salad, oven-fresh bread sticks with marinara sauce, and choose your toppings wood-fired pizza. Vegetarian options available. HOW MANYFrom 2 to about 70 guests per day. Minimum price required for private tour to run. Public Tours available.

title: Electric Bike/Train Coastal Tour
description: Want to ride the rails and explore on your electric or road bike? The tour begins at the Solana Beach Train Station where you will board the train, with your bike, headed to Carlsbad Village. The train fare of $5 is included in your tour charge. The train follows the coastline, so you can enjoy spectacular views along the way. Once off the train in Carlsbad, your bike adventure will begin. As you ride back to Solana Beach you will enjoy incredible coastline vistas, white sand beaches, meditation gardens, local history, and restaurants to experience for lunch. The bike riding leg of the tour is about 16 miles with up to 14 stops along the way. This tour is a cell phone, narrated and guided tour app. The map on your phone will show you the stops and the route we recommend. It will also show your position along the route, so you won't get lost. When you get close to a scheduled stop, your app tour guide will share information, history, and stories with you. Do not forget your ear buds or earphones for the best audio enjoyment. If you need to rent an electric bike for the tour please contact Pedego Solana Beach at 858.764.2979 or you can make an online reservation at pedegosolanabeach.com. Their rental bikes come with a cell phone holder on the handlebar and a standard USB port to keep your phone charged from the bike's power source. Bring your phone charging cord to connect with the bike. The bike rental is not included in the tour cost. This experience should not be rushed, so plan to start in the morning and enjoy the entire day. Fun train ride, incredible coastline views, tasty food, no traffic or parking hassles, and fun in the sun. Most of the tour you will be riding in a bike lane on a road with automobiles.

title: La Jolla Tour - Sip, Savor and Sea
description: The Sip, Savor and Sea tour is a great way to explore the coastal town of La Jolla with a professional tour guide. La Jolla is known as the "Jewel of San Diego" with some much marine wildlife, art, and historic figures who have paved the way in the world of science and medicine. Learn about their stories, enjoy seaside views, and walk along the village streets lined with galleries and trendy restaurants. What to Expect: A guided walking tour of La Jolla Village & Cove, weaving together scenic oceanfront views, local history, culinary tastings, and art. The walk covers roughly ½ to ¾ mile, including a downhill segment to reach the Cove—so comfy shoes are a must. Culinary & Culinary Highlights: Enjoy a savory food and drink tasting at a local restaurant, often featuring gourmet tacos and margaritas—Puesto is one favored stop. Wrap up on a sweet note with dessert, typically artisanal gelato from a local French/Italian-style bakery. Cultural and Scenic Stops: Visit an art gallery, led by a curator, offering a glimpse into the local contemporary art scene. Stroll past iconic landmarks like Sunny Jim's Sea Cave, a quirky and historic cave accessed via the famous Cave Store. At the picturesque La Jolla Cove, spot sea lions basking on rocks and take in sweeping ocean views. Storytelling & Local Flavor: Guides share engaging stories about La Jolla's history, with a special nod to pioneering figures—especially women—who helped shape the area's rich cultural and scientific landscape. You'll also hear fun local anecdotes—about Hollywood-era hangouts, marine mysteries, and more.

title: Cin! Cin! (Cheers!): Wine Tour
description: A guided tour through the flavors and aromas of Little Italy. Planning a romantic date or your next friend's night out? Our Little Italy wine tasting tour is the perfect way to elevate your outing and get to know one of San Diego's most unique downtown neighborhoods. As we stroll through this historic fishing town, you'll learn all about the area's rich history and, of course, sample the food and award winning wine that makes Little Italy one of the best dining locations in all of San Diego and, dare we say, Southern California. About Wine Tasting In Little ItalyVino! Vino! is a walking San Diego wine tour and a great way to get to know Little Italy, San Diego - one of the chicest neighborhoods in the Downtown area. Meet with your tour guide and head on to your culinary flavor adventure. With many unique boutiques, historic buildings, and luxury high-rises, this area is known today as a great area to stay and find some of the best food and drink establishments in all of San Diego. On this fine wine tour, you will visit 3 establishments and sample a variety of premium wines from Southern California wine country and beyond. You will meet local wine enthusiasts who are eager to share with you small production wines, as well as Italian favorites. Spend the day learning about the different styles of great wine, and taste your way through Little Italy. Taste not just great wine but Italian foodie favorites such as Italian cheeses and a favorite pasta dish. A walking history tour of San Diego's own Little Italy Explore the sights, sounds, smells and flavors of this chic Downtown neighborhood Learn about unique premium wines from California, Italy and other parts of the world Get to know some of San Diego's biggest wine fanatics at 3 venues Important Information: All guests must bring valid ID with them for the wine tour. Days/Times Available*: Friday through Sunday at 3:00pm Rate (Age 21+): $125 per Person Duration: 3 Hours Minimum Guests: 4 / Maximum Guests: 12

title: San Diego Bay Parade of Lights
description: Make your spirits bright at the San Diego Bay Parade of Lights, a time-honored holiday tradition brought to San Diego by the boating community. The parade features over 80 lavishly decorated boats sailing in the bay hoping to win a prize for originality and creativity. Gather with friends, family, and over 100,000 others on the shores of San Diego Bay for one of the most iconic events in the region.

title: Meditation
description: This meditation session is designed for adults to immerse themselves in relaxation and tranquility through guided techniques such as breathwork, visualization, and mindfulness exercises. The program is led by Hema Singh and/or Sheetal Gupta, both experienced practitioners with backgrounds in leading meditation sessions and participating in mental health campaigns. The event provides a dedicated adult-only space for enhancing overall well-being.

title: Lamb's Festival of Christmas: Fargo North of Normal
description: Experience the heartwarming magic of Lamb's Festival of Christmas at Lamb's Players Theatre in Coronado, California, where a diverse group of characters—including a rising star, a real estate mogul, a spirited child, and an octogenarian—find themselves vying for the same dream home just in time for the holidays. This delightful production weaves together comedic twists, touching moments, and beloved seasonal music, creating a joyful celebration for all ages. With its engaging story and festive spirit, the show has become a cherished holiday tradition for audiences in San Diego. Discover why families return year after year to share in the laughter and warmth of this unique theatrical event.

title: The Cagematch
description: The Cagematch is an improv comedy event where two teams compete on stage with quick wit and improvisational skills. The audience participates by deciding which team advances to the next showdown through their reactions. The event is designed as an interactive and energetic improv battle.

title: A Christmas Story
description: A Christmas Story is a nostalgic musical about nine-year-old Ralphie Parker, who wants an Official Red Ryder Range Model Carbine Action BB Gun for Christmas. Despite warnings that he will shoot his eye out, Ralphie tries to convince everyone, from his mother to Santa Claus, that he needs the gun. The show features showstopping numbers, tap routines, and quirky characters, capturing the spirit of the holiday season.

title: Torrey Pines Hike + Beach Picnic
description: Escape the city and reconnect with nature on this refreshing 3-hour experience along the San Diego coastline. Our Torrey Pines Hiking + Picnic tour combines a light guided hike through one of Southern California's most iconic natural reserves with a relaxing beachside picnic.​Led by a knowledgeable local guide, explore the coastal cliffs, wild landscapes, and native plant life of Torrey Pines State Natural Reserve. After the hike, descend to the beach for a peaceful picnic with ocean views—perfect for photos, relaxation, and rejuvenation before heading back to downtown San Diego. Led by an experienced local guide, you'll explore: Dramatic oceanfront cliffs Sculpted sandstone canyons Unique native plant life, including the rare Torrey pine tree Panoramic views of the Pacific Ocean (and seasonal whale sightings!) Insights into the reserve's rich geology and ecology After your hike, descend to Torrey Pines State Beach, where a gourmet picnic box lunch awaits. Here, your group can relax by the shore, dip your feet in the ocean, and enjoy the soothing sounds of the waves in a peaceful, uncrowded setting. 3 Hours |Includes: Trail guide, picnic box lunch and beach relaxation Includes:​​ Private round-trip transportation Expert bilingual trail guide Guided hike through Torrey Pines State Natural Reserve Gourmet picnic box lunch at the beach Bottled water and beach blanket setup

title: Stay Sharp with Games
description: This event focuses on preserving mental abilities through stimulating activities such as playing games and learning new skills. It is designed to help keep the brain healthy as people age. The program is held on the second and fourth Mondays of each month and welcomes adults of all ages.

title: Tinker Tuesdays
description: Tinker Tuesdays is a recurring program held at the Mission Hills-Hillcrest Library, where participants can bring their ideas to life using various maker lab equipment. The event features tools such as 3D printers, a laser cutter, and a die-cut machine powered by Silhouette Studio. It is open to all ages, with some equipment requiring an appointment.

title: Baja Pennisula Grand Adventure Tour
description: Explore the stunning landscapes of Baja California with our 8-day adventure, featuring top beaches, bustling cities, historic missions, and incredible wildlife. As the only American company fully licensed to operate this Baja California tour, Five Star Tours offers expert guidance with bilingual-speaking guides for an immersive experience. Book now to discover the best of Baja California's secluded beauty with unmatched expertise and comfort!Starting at $ 3080 USD per person based on double accommodation Length: 8 Days Baja Experience Overview Experience the best of Baja California and Baja California Sur with our expertly guided tours, departing from either San Diego or Cabo San Lucas. Enjoy seamless transportation, comfortable hotel accommodations, several meals, and exciting activities all included. This is the ultimate way to explore Mexico's breathtaking landscapes and vibrant culture. Consult our detailed itinerary today and start planning your unforgettable Baja adventure!​Prices**Single occupancy:** $ 3520 per guest ( one guest per room)**Double occupancy:** $3080 per guest ( two guests per room)**Triple occupancy:** $ 2860 per guest ( three guest per room)**Quadruple occupancy:** $ 2530 per guest ( four guest per room) include transportation, guide, hotel, boat fees and most meals and activities noted on the trip itinerary Dates & Prices

title: California Tower Tours
description: Join the Museum of Us for a uniquely-San Diego experience! Built in 1915, the California Tower is one of San Diego's most iconic historic landmarks. Make the climb on a guided tour that highlights the origins of Balboa Park and the histories of some of the many diverse communities that call the region home. All tours culminate in breathtaking 360-degree views of San Diego from the Tower's eighth-floor viewing deck—from the Pacific Ocean in the west to the Cuyamaca Mountains in the east, and beyond. California Tower tours are offered daily when the Museum is open, per the schedule below: Monday-Thursday: 10:30 a.m., 12:30 p.m., 2:30 p.m. and 4:30 p.m. Friday-Sunday: Hourly from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m.

title: Trivia
description: Trivia at Brewery X is a weekly trivia night event held at Brewery X, located on Harbor Island in San Diego. Participants can test their knowledge and compete for prizes. The event occurs every Wednesday evening from 7:00 PM to 9:00 PM throughout most of 2026.

title: San Diego City and Coastline Daily Tour
description: Perfect for first-time visitors and curious locals alike, our San Diego Highlights Daily Tour is a personalized 4-hour journey showcasing the best of America's Finest City. Travel in comfort with your own private driver-guide as you explore San Diego's most iconic neighborhoods and breathtaking coastal views.Begin in the historic Gaslamp Quarter, known for its Victorian-era architecture and vibrant nightlife. Cruise past Petco Park and the modern skyline before heading to Balboa Park, home to over 16 museums, lush gardens, and the world-famous San Diego Zoo.​Cross the scenic Coronado Bridge to explore the charming island of Coronado, where you'll admire the iconic Hotel del Coronado and white-sand beaches. Then, journey north along the coast to the upscale seaside village of La Jolla, where you'll spot sea lions basking on the rocks and stop at the panoramic Mt. Soledad National Veterans Memorial, offering sweeping 360° views of the Pacific Ocean, San Diego skyline, and even Mexico on clear days.

title: Flavors of Little Italy: Food Tour
description: No San Diego Little Italy walking tour would be complete without exploring this culinary hotspot's best places to eat. Known for its variety of restaurants, America's biggest Little Italy has become a food lover's bucket list destination, and we're here to show it off! Join our Little Italy walking tour full of food, fun, and Italian culture. Little Italy is an Italian enclave of great culinary delights in downtown San Diego, and its vibrant atmosphere can be observed during a city tour. San Diego's Italian quarter has grown in popularity for not just Italian fare, but also for the rich array of local restaurant hot-spots that have opened up in the last 10 years, or so. In addition to the traditional pasta and pizza dishes, Little Italy offers up top chef cuisine, vegan breakfasts, juicy burgers, and lounges for dancing and fine cocktails! Consider every day a Little Italy food fest! The streets of Little Italy are lined with Italian American staples, making the Little Italy Tour a must-try experience among travelers visiting San Diego. The possibilities to whet your palate are endless. After all, what better way to savor the delicious food paired with sangria, beer, or wine tasting. Our Little Italy tours will have you savoring iconic bites while you get an understanding of the history and current vibe of the neighborhood with one of our professional and personable tour guides. A walking tour of the exciting history of Little Italy Taste a combination of Italian flavors and unique bites from 4 restaurants that represent the area Learn about the early fishing industry of San Diego Meet some of the local Italians and Italian immigrants who've been a part of the revitalization of the area Wine, sangria or local beer tasters Days/Times Available*: Monday through Thursday at 4:30pm Rate (Age 13+): $88 per Person Duration: 2.5 Hours Minimum Guests: 4 / Maximum Guests: 15

title: ALL-NEW Shark Encounter
description: ALL-NEW Shark Encounter to Open May 22, 2026 Come face-to-fin with some of the ocean's most fascinating predators in a newly refreshed exhibit: Encounter mesmerizing new shark species alongside a vibrant array of marine life—including additional sharks and colorful fish—as the expanded exhibit transforms into a dynamic underwater adventure. Experience the ocean like never before in a breathtakingly enhanced space that pulls guests into an immersive realm—celebrating the beauty and power of sharks while challenging long-held myths about this extraordinary species. The all-new Shark Encounter steps beyond traditional fear-based portrayals to educate guests about the incredible diversity of sharks—over 400 species worldwide—highlighting their varied sizes, shapes, and fascinating adaptations that contribute to healthy oceans. This is more than a refresh—it's a commitment: The reimagined experience continues to showcase the stunning array of sharks, with 11 different species including sand tiger sharks, blacktip reef sharks, and the endangered Australian leopard shark—part of a Species Survival Plan through AZA-accredited facilities. Almost all the species at Shark Encounter are listed as at least vulnerable or near threatened, reinforcing SeaWorld's commitment to education and species protection. Stunning visual upgrades and immersive storytelling: State-of-the-art multimedia brings the new experience to life, fusing striking visuals with education-driven storytelling to deepen the connection between humans and one of the ocean's most iconic predators. An overhead video ceiling in the entry hallway features sharks swimming above, guiding guests toward the underwater tunnel. In the finale, a multi-screen installation surrounds you with rare live footage of shark diversity in a mesmerizing 8-minute presentation. Exclusive VIP Shark Tour: As part of the all-new experience, adventurers will have the chance to learn up-close how our zoological specialists feed and care for the sharks. The tour includes access to a brand-new walkway above the shark habitat, offering a dramatic, elevated view of these fascinating animals from a unique perspective. This unforgettable experience promotes deeper understanding, personal connection, and marine life conservation. Purchase details coming soon!

title: Petco Park Tours
description: Experience Petco Park like never before with a behind-the-scenes ballpark tour! Guests will have the opportunity to explore exclusive areas of the stadium, learn about the history of the San Diego Padres, and take in one of baseball's most iconic venues from a unique perspective. Tours are available on select dates throughout the season; however, tours do not operate on certain event days, holidays, and select game or stadium blackout dates. On concert days, tour times may be adjusted to accommodate event operations. Tours are currently not available on the following dates. This list is subject to change. 2026: March 7 (San Diego Science Expo) March 21-22 (Savannah Bananas) April 30 (Full Ballpark Event) May 13-16 (Full Ballpark Event) June 11-14 (Field of Dreamz Festival) June 16-19 (Fuerza Rediga) July 19-22 (Ed Sheeran Concert) August 13-16 (Rufus Du Sol) August 15-18 (Noah Kahan) August 28-31 (My Chemical Romance Concert) September 10-13 (Pierce the Veil) On Gallagher Square concerts days, we adjust our tour schedule as follows: Weekday: The 2:00 PM tour will not be available Weekends: The 1:00 PM and 2:00 PM tours will not be available Please refer to the Petco Park event schedule to view upcoming concert dates. For the most up-to-date tour schedule, blackout dates, and adjusted concert tour times, please visit the Padres Tours page.

title: Fight Inflation on This Elevated Wine Tasting
description: This event offers a VIP wine tasting experience at a low price, including door-to-door transportation, ample wine pours, a wine tour, and a winery meal. It features local San Diego County wineries and provides a fully guided adventure with entertaining commentary on local history and viticulture. The tours run from Wednesday through Sunday for groups ranging from 2 to 100 guests, welcoming single guests as well.

title: San Diego Beer, Wine, and Spirits
description: Head to the original California wine country for a memorable tasting experience. Explore the natural beauty of the vineyards while savoring the unique flavors of the region's world-class wines.

title: Klara Chavarria Art Gallery
description: The Klara Chavarria Art Gallery showcases the captivating work of artist Klara Chavarría, featuring a vibrant collection of internationally acclaimed abstract and figurative expressionism. The gallery offers a profound exploration of human emotions and existence through contemporary fine art. The event recurs monthly on the first Friday through January 1, 2027, providing an ongoing opportunity to experience Klara's unique artistic vision.

title: Old Town Trolley’s Holiday Sights and Festive Nights Tour
description: Old Town Trolley’s Holiday Sights and Festive Nights Tour in San Diego invites families and friends to experience the city’s festive spirit from the comfort of a decorated trolley. Guests are treated to cheerful holiday music, candy canes, and interactive sing-alongs with lyric books, making the ride lively and engaging. Knowledgeable guides share fun stories and lead playful games, creating a warm and memorable holiday outing.

title: A Very Furry Christmas
description: A Very Furry Christmas at Sesame Place San Diego transforms the park into a cheerful holiday wonderland, inviting families to celebrate the season with beloved Sesame Street characters. Guests can enjoy festive shows, holiday-themed attractions, and interactive experiences designed for all ages. The event features dazzling decorations and plenty of opportunities for memorable photos with favorite furry friends. It’s a joyful way to create lasting holiday memories in a playful, welcoming setting.

title: Shelter Island Farmers Market
description: Shelter Island Friday Farmers Market is a weekly waterfront market held every Friday at the Island Palms Hotel and Marina. The market features local artisans, makers, and small businesses offering fresh sourdough, honey, desserts, clothing, skincare, resin art, hats, bags, hot tea, and more. Visitors can enjoy the market's laid-back atmosphere with beautiful Marina views and island vibes while supporting local vendors.

title: LEGO® Galaxy NOW OPEN at LEGOLAND® California
description: LEGO® Galaxy is an ALL-NEW space-themed land at LEGOLAND® California where families can launch into a world of imagination, creativity, and adventure inspired by decades of LEGO space stories. Guests can explore an immersive environment filled with impressive brick built space models, interactive experiences, and character meet and greets. The land brings together classic and modern LEGO space themes, inviting explorers of all ages to discover distant worlds, train like astronauts, and see LEGO creativity taken to a whole new universe. At the heart of LEGO Galaxy is a collection of adventures designed for the whole family, including Galacticoaster, an indoor family coaster, along with additional space inspired rides and a dedicated toddler play zone. Guests can build and test LEGO rockets, enjoy hands on play areas, and refuel with galactic themed food and treats before stopping by Orbital Outpost to pick up space ready souvenirs. With rides, dining, shopping, and playful storytelling all in one place, LEGO Galaxy offers an out of this world experience for young space explorers and their families. Now Open since March 6th, 2026

title: Midday Sail
description: This is what sailing in San Diego is all about! Hop aboard one of our ticketed midday sails or book the whole boat just for you and your crew! If you've got four or more passengers, a private sail is definitely the way to go, and you'll have the whole boat to yourself! Ticketed vs. Private: Ticketed sails are great for solo adventurers, couples, and groups of 4 or less. Private sails are highly recommended for families and groups of 4+. Enjoy 3 hours of sailing during the best time of the day! More info: Peak Sailing Conditions: The wind's up, the boat's moving, and you're getting the real sailing experience. Wildlife in Action: Midday is prime time for spotting dolphins, sea lions, and maybe even a whale. San Diego at Its Best: Clear skies, wide-open water, and views you won't get shoreside. Plenty of Time for More: A solid adventure without killing the rest of your day. Captains Who Know Their Stuff: USCG-certified, super experienced, and actually fun to hang out with. Cold Drinks Included: Beer, hard seltzer, soft drinks, and snacks — all on the house.

title: Sunset Sail
description: Nothing beats a San Diego sunset, except for a San Diego Sunset Cruise. Experience the best views of golden hour and downtown San Diego from the deck of a sailboat on the San Diego Bay. A Sunset Cruise Worth Slowing Down For: No traffic or noisy bars, and nothing between you and the horizon. Perfect For Date Night (or Any Night): Anniversaries, birthdays, or just an excuse to cuddle up with your boo. Small Group Sailing Experience: There's no such thing as a bad view from the deck of our premium sailboats. Captains Who Know Their Stuff: USCG-certified, super experienced, and actually fun to hang out with. Start Your Evening with a Toast: We've got beer, hard seltzer, soft drinks, and snacks waiting on board for you. Book a Ticketed Sunset Sail or Private Sunset Sail. Ticketed charters are capped at 6 passengers max; private boats can be booked for groups up to 12 passengers.

title: Large Group Sails
description: This is how locals sail San Diego. This vessel is ideal for larger groups, featuring a spacious deck for relaxing or socializing, a full galley below deck, two staterooms, and two bathrooms on board for your comfort and convenience. This sailboat can comfortably accommodate groups up to 22 passengers Includes drinks (waters and sodas), snacks, and experienced captain + crew on board Optional: Upgrade your sail with a bar package Bar Package: The bar package features a selection of lagers, hard seltzers, and a variety of wines for you to enjoy, including white wine and rosé. Customize your charter: you choose your departure time and if you would like to sail for 2,3 or 4 hours. It's your private sailboat! Perfect for large gatherings, birthdays, family reunions, small weddings, and corporate events.

title: Painted Prayers: The Japanese Folk Art Tradition of Ema
description: The Painted Prayers exhibition highlights the Japanese folk painting tradition of ema, which are house-shaped wooden votive plaques used as prayers in Shinto shrines and Buddhist temples. The exhibition presents a variety of ema, ranging from the 18th century to the present day, showcasing their colorful imagery and the prayers associated with them. Curated by Japanese art historian Meher McArthur, this is the first museum exhibition in the United States to display such an extensive collection of ema.

title: 10% Off Mon-Thurs Intimate Whale Watching Tours
description: Experience the thrill of being up close to nature on a small, fast, rigid inflatable boat! We glide through the ocean on a military decommissioned boat on a wildlife safari to see whales, dolphins, sea-lions, and so much more! Our boats are low profile which allows us to get arms reach away from wildlife. On our educational, guided tours every passenger gets to enjoy 360-degree, front row views and one-on-one time to talk to the captain. With no crowds, no hassle, and a personalized tour, there is no question why Adventure Whale Watching has been given 5-star ratings by almost every explorer! With small group sizes we are able to personalize every tour to give each of our guests their own unique and memorable experience! Join us for the adventure of a lifetime! Make your reservation today!Adventurewhalewatchingsandiego.com(619) 808-2822 Who we are: At Adventure Whale Watching we specialize in fast-moving, fun boat tours on Navy Seal Rigid Inflatable Boats (R.I.B.s). We are one of the only year-round Whale & Dolphin Watching companies in San Diego! Our boats set us apart because they are low profile which creates stability so it eliminates the aggressive rocking that you experience on the bigger, slow-moving boats. Our boats are also faster than typical whale watching tours which means that we are able to cover more area allowing us to see more and get closer to the wildlife. We have a small number of passengers per boat which will ensure that the boat is never crowded and that each of the guests gets a personalized experience with 360 degree views from every seat! Educational: Our captains are also naturalists and marine biologists who will educate you about the wildlife, the boats, and give you some history about San Diego! We have NOAA Marine Mammal Identification Guides we use to help passengers learn about and identify the potential species we can see on the tour. We also have tide charts and Boating Safety Information. We also talk about the Marine Mammal Protection Act, Entanglements, and Environmental Conservation. We may even pick up some balloons or water bottles if we find garbage in the ocean to help our marine friends!Safety: Our tours are guided by United States Coast Guard certified captains and naturalists aboard military decommissioned Rigid Inflatable Boats (R.I.B.s). These low-profile boats are some of the safest boats on the water! Additionally, we offer each passenger a life vest and the choice of a snow suit or a military survival suit to keep them warm and dry while on the boat. Join Us! Winter excursions, between December 15th & March 31st, are offered daily at 8:30am, 11:30am, and 2:30pm. Summer excursions, between April 1st & October 14th, are offered daily at 8:30, 12:00pm, & 3:30pm. Fall excursions, between October 15th & December 14th, are offered daily at 8:30 & 12:00pm. Pricing: The price for 2.5 - 3 hour Public Whale Watching tours are $129 plus tax per guest. Private Whale Watching tours are offered for $775 plus tax for up to 6 passengers. Use code WEEKDAY10 for 10% Monday - Thursday tours! Please call to accommodate groups larger than 6. Reservations are required.

title: Skate Craft
description: Skate Craft celebrates skateboarding's do-it-yourself values, tracing its evolution from handmade decks and ramps to contemporary handcrafted art. The exhibition highlights the creativity, self-expression, and craftsmanship involved in skateboard design, safety gear, and skate shoes. It presents skateboarding as a form of living folk art that combines play, design, and sustainability.

title: Alma Unleashed: A Canine Social
description: Unwind with your pup at Alma Unleashed: A Canine Social—a daily happy hour at the Lobby Bar & Patio. Bring your dog and enjoy 20% off select food and beverages from 4:00-6:00 PM, where great drinks and good dogs go hand in hand. Must be 21+ to consume alcoholic beverages. Must bring dog to redeem discount. Not valid with any other offer or discount. Dogs must be leashed and supervised at all times. Management reserves all rights.

title: Alma Unleashed: A Canine Social
description: Unwind with your pup at Alma Unleashed: A Canine Social—a daily happy hour at the Lobby Bar & Patio. Bring your dog and enjoy 20% off select food and beverages from 4:00-6:00 PM, where great drinks and good dogs go hand in hand. Must be 21+ to consume alcoholic beverages. Must bring dog to redeem discount. Not valid with any other offer or discount. Dogs must be leashed and supervised at all times. Management reserves all rights.

title: Take Me to the Water: Histories of the Black Pacific Exhibit
description: Opening Saturday, May 23 • Memorial Day Weekend 2026Take Me to the Water: Histories of the Black Pacific opens at the Maritime Museum of San Diego and is included with general admission. Take Me to the Water: Histories of the Black Pacific explores the deep and often overlooked relationships between Black communities and the Pacific Ocean. Spanning centuries of maritime movement, labor, exploration, and cultural exchange, the exhibit highlights the lives and contributions of Black mariners whose stories helped shape the Pacific world. Visitors are invited to discover the experiences of sailors, whalers, fishers, waterfront workers, explorers, soldiers, surfers, swimmers, and scholars whose connections to the ocean expanded far beyond traditional narratives of maritime history. Through immersive storytelling, historical interpretation, and contemporary scholarship, the exhibit offers a broader and more inclusive understanding of the Pacific—revealing how identity, labor, migration, resilience, and culture moved across oceans and generations. Developed in partnership with UC San Diego curator Dr. Caroline Collins, Take Me to the Water: Histories of the Black Pacific brings together research, storytelling, and maritime history to explore Black relationships with the Pacific Ocean and the broader maritime world. The exhibit invites visitors to reconsider familiar narratives of exploration, labor, and seafaring through stories that have too often remained outside traditional maritime histories.

title: Happy Hour at the Coop!
description: Enjoy an unbeatable happy hour with bites starting at $2, plus beer & cocktail specials.

title: Electric Bike/Train Coastal Tour
description: Want to ride the rails and explore on your electric or road bike? The tour begins at the Solana Beach Train Station where you will board the train, with your bike, headed to Carlsbad Village. The train fare of $5 is included in your tour charge. The train follows the coastline, so you can enjoy spectacular views along the way. Once off the train in Carlsbad, your bike adventure will begin. As you ride back to Solana Beach you will enjoy incredible coastline vistas, white sand beaches, meditation gardens, local history, and restaurants to experience for lunch. The bike riding leg of the tour is about 16 miles with up to 14 stops along the way. This tour is a cell phone, narrated and guided tour app. The map on your phone will show you the stops and the route we recommend. It will also show your position along the route, so you won't get lost. When you get close to a scheduled stop, your app tour guide will share information, history, and stories with you. Do not forget your ear buds or earphones for the best audio enjoyment. If you need to rent an electric bike for the tour please contact Pedego Solana Beach at 858.764.2979 or you can make an online reservation at pedegosolanabeach.com. Their rental bikes come with a cell phone holder on the handlebar and a standard USB port to keep your phone charged from the bike's power source. Bring your phone charging cord to connect with the bike. The bike rental is not included in the tour cost. This experience should not be rushed, so plan to start in the morning and enjoy the entire day. Fun train ride, incredible coastline views, tasty food, no traffic or parking hassles, and fun in the sun. Most of the tour you will be riding in a bike lane on a road with automobiles.

title: La Jolla Tour - Sip, Savor and Sea
description: The Sip, Savor and Sea tour is a great way to explore the coastal town of La Jolla with a professional tour guide. La Jolla is known as the "Jewel of San Diego" with some much marine wildlife, art, and historic figures who have paved the way in the world of science and medicine. Learn about their stories, enjoy seaside views, and walk along the village streets lined with galleries and trendy restaurants. What to Expect: A guided walking tour of La Jolla Village & Cove, weaving together scenic oceanfront views, local history, culinary tastings, and art. The walk covers roughly ½ to ¾ mile, including a downhill segment to reach the Cove—so comfy shoes are a must. Culinary & Culinary Highlights: Enjoy a savory food and drink tasting at a local restaurant, often featuring gourmet tacos and margaritas—Puesto is one favored stop. Wrap up on a sweet note with dessert, typically artisanal gelato from a local French/Italian-style bakery. Cultural and Scenic Stops: Visit an art gallery, led by a curator, offering a glimpse into the local contemporary art scene. Stroll past iconic landmarks like Sunny Jim's Sea Cave, a quirky and historic cave accessed via the famous Cave Store. At the picturesque La Jolla Cove, spot sea lions basking on rocks and take in sweeping ocean views. Storytelling & Local Flavor: Guides share engaging stories about La Jolla's history, with a special nod to pioneering figures—especially women—who helped shape the area's rich cultural and scientific landscape. You'll also hear fun local anecdotes—about Hollywood-era hangouts, marine mysteries, and more.

title: Happy Hour
description: Available weekdays at our bar and front dining room! Featuring deals on wine and sangria, along with our best-selling burger, truffle parm papas, hummus, and croquettes. *Happy Hour food specials are subject to change.*

title: Nutcracker: Magical Christmas Ballet
description: Experience the enchantment of the Nutcracker: Magical Christmas Ballet, a heartwarming holiday spectacle with an international cast, whimsical puppetry, and lavish costumes and sets crafted by Europe's finest artisans. Set to Tchaikovsky's timeless score, this beloved Nutcracker tradition promises a Christmas memory that families will treasure forever.

title: Meditation
description: This meditation session is designed for adults to immerse themselves in relaxation and tranquility through guided techniques such as breathwork, visualization, and mindfulness exercises. The program is led by Hema Singh and/or Sheetal Gupta, both experienced practitioners with backgrounds in leading meditation sessions and participating in mental health campaigns. The event provides a dedicated adult-only space for enhancing overall well-being.

title: Lamb's Festival of Christmas: Fargo North of Normal
description: Experience the heartwarming magic of Lamb's Festival of Christmas at Lamb's Players Theatre in Coronado, California, where a diverse group of characters—including a rising star, a real estate mogul, a spirited child, and an octogenarian—find themselves vying for the same dream home just in time for the holidays. This delightful production weaves together comedic twists, touching moments, and beloved seasonal music, creating a joyful celebration for all ages. With its engaging story and festive spirit, the show has become a cherished holiday tradition for audiences in San Diego. Discover why families return year after year to share in the laughter and warmth of this unique theatrical event.

title: The Cagematch
description: The Cagematch is an improv comedy event where two teams compete on stage with quick wit and improvisational skills. The audience participates by deciding which team advances to the next showdown through their reactions. The event is designed as an interactive and energetic improv battle.

title: A Christmas Story
description: A Christmas Story is a nostalgic musical about nine-year-old Ralphie Parker, who wants an Official Red Ryder Range Model Carbine Action BB Gun for Christmas. Despite warnings that he will shoot his eye out, Ralphie tries to convince everyone, from his mother to Santa Claus, that he needs the gun. The show features showstopping numbers, tap routines, and quirky characters, capturing the spirit of the holiday season.

title: Stay Sharp with Games
description: This event focuses on preserving mental abilities through stimulating activities such as playing games and learning new skills. It is designed to help keep the brain healthy as people age. The program is held on the second and fourth Mondays of each month and welcomes adults of all ages.

title: Ensenada Pacific Coastline Tour from San Diego
description: Discover the coastal beauty and cultural charm of Ensenada on this full-day adventure from San Diego. From the bustling seafood black market to oceanfront promenades and colorful local shops, this experience captures the heart of Baja California's most beloved port city. Ideal for travelers seeking a relaxed yet immersive cultural escape, this tour offers a perfect blend of flavors, history, and scenic ocean views. Tour Highlights: Scenic drive along the Pacific Coast from San Diego to Ensenada Visit the famous Seafood Black Market (Mercado Negro) for fresh local catches Stroll along El Malecón, Ensenada's iconic seaside promenade Explore La Calle Primera, filled with local shops, handicrafts, and souvenirs Free time for local shopping and optional lunch at a traditional seafood restaurant Border-crossing assistance and bilingual guide throughout the tour Ensenada Pacific Coastline Tour from San DiegoDuration: 10-12 hours Includes:​​​ Round-trip private transportation from San Diego Bilingual tour guide Guided visit to main landmarks and local markets Bottled water on board ​Not Included:​​​​​ Meals and drinks Personal purchases or souvenirs Gratuities for guide and driver

title: San Diego Chasing Sunsets Tour
description: Discover San Diego's breathtaking beauty during golden hour on our exclusive Chasing Sunsets Tour—a 4-hour journey along the Pacific Coast designed to capture the most magical moments of the day. Begin in a picturesque seaside town such as La Jolla or Ocean Beach, and let our expert local guide take you through a curated route of San Diego's top coastal viewpoints. Stops:​ Sunset Cliffs Natural Park - dramatic cliffs and crashing waves Mt. Soledad - sweeping panoramic views of the entire city Windansea Beach - a local favorite with textured rock formations Secret coastal parks and off-the-beaten-path lookouts 3 Hours |Includes: guide, view points, wine and olive oil tasting stop and viewpoints. Includes:​ Private round-trip transportation from your location Local San Diego guide Stops at 3-4 top sunset viewpoints Bottled water and light snacks Time for walking, exploring, and photo opportunities ​Not Included: Gratuities Meals unless picnic upgrade is selected Personal expenses

title: Tinker Tuesdays
description: Tinker Tuesdays is a recurring program held at the Mission Hills-Hillcrest Library, where participants can bring their ideas to life using various maker lab equipment. The event features tools such as 3D printers, a laser cutter, and a die-cut machine powered by Silhouette Studio. It is open to all ages, with some equipment requiring an appointment.

title: California Tower Tours
description: Join the Museum of Us for a uniquely-San Diego experience! Built in 1915, the California Tower is one of San Diego's most iconic historic landmarks. Make the climb on a guided tour that highlights the origins of Balboa Park and the histories of some of the many diverse communities that call the region home. All tours culminate in breathtaking 360-degree views of San Diego from the Tower's eighth-floor viewing deck—from the Pacific Ocean in the west to the Cuyamaca Mountains in the east, and beyond. California Tower tours are offered daily when the Museum is open, per the schedule below: Monday-Thursday: 10:30 a.m., 12:30 p.m., 2:30 p.m. and 4:30 p.m. Friday-Sunday: Hourly from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m.

title: Trivia
description: Trivia at Brewery X is a weekly trivia night event held at Brewery X, located on Harbor Island in San Diego. Participants can test their knowledge and compete for prizes. The event occurs every Wednesday evening from 7:00 PM to 9:00 PM throughout most of 2026.

title: Liberty Station: From Barracks to Brews
description: Calling all new recruits! Join a local guide and get an inside look at what it was like to join the navy without a mulit-year commitment! Learn some sailor skills, explore the buildings essential to training, learn fun facts about the recruit experience, and gain an understanding of the history of the Naval Training Center. Tour Highlights Learn important sailor skills without having to drop and give us 20 Explore recruit life by visiting buildings essential to training such as barracks, the mess hall, and other facilities Using historic photos, we'll compare the recruit experience with the modern-day visitor's experience Celebrate the completion of your "training" by raising a brew (or comparable beverage) PRIVATE TOURS AVAILABLE; CLICK "TICKETS" FOR DETAILS Itinerary We will kick things off by exploring the heart of what was once Camp Paul Jones, the first camp established that housed 100-150 recruits per barracks and get a feel for what it was like to be one of those sailors (without the drill instructor!). Its park-like setting is full of secrets - such as the history of the large tree there and the special requirements for decorating it at Christmas, the design of the barracks' roof that's key to keeping it cool, or the connection the current public art on display has with the camp's Navy past. Then pass by the former dispensary, where early recruits received medical care - sometimes for the first time in their lives. We'll view the former Sail Ho Golf Course because it wasn't all work and no play on the base. Several golf pros have played here, and the course is even a final resting place for two people! At the historic entrance, we get a feel for what it was like to enter the base as a new recruit, hear how the gate played a role in their daily lives, and what impact having the Marine Core Training Center had on Naval recruits. It wasn't all men who served here, at least not after 1943 when WAVES (Women Accepted for Volunteer Emergency Service) arrived, taking up residence in a nearby barracks away from the men. We'll then pass by the very first buildings on base which contained the Mess Hall and talk about what it took to feed so many people, multiple times a day. We'll pause at a work of art that replicates the whale boat sailors learned to row in the nearby channel and learn a sailor skill or two. Afterward we'll head over to the heart of the base - the Command Center - where the top brass kept the base running. There we'll view an exhibit that highlights recruit life including something dear to everyone - food! From there it's on to Ingram Plaza, to learn about the person it was named for, and why that's a better name than its alternative. Next up we'll visit the Boats Memorial, dedicated to the 52 submarines and their crews lost during World War II. If time and energy allow, we'll walk to the landlocked ship referred to by locals as the "USS Neversail." Then we'll proceed across Preble Field, where recruits marched during training and on their graduation day. We'll celebrate their legacy and your "graduation" from the tour and sailor training with a beverage near where we started. From there, your guide will recommend additional ways to explore the Navy's legacy at Liberty Station, along with how to enjoy the area's many cultural organizations or provide information on its many restaurants.

title: Secrets and Highlights of Balboa Park
description: Balboa Park is more than just a beautiful place — it's a living museum of San Diego's story. Join us for a walk filled with art, architecture, and nature, all brought to life with fun and surprising details. Tour Highlights Discover the inspiring, quirky, and fascinating history of one of the nation's largest urban cultural parksUncover historical and architectural treasures hiding in plain sightExplore a botanical garden's vibrant collection.Learn about the early 20th century expositions that shaped Balboa ParkFind the best spots to photograph Balboa Park's beautiful Spanish colonial revival architecture PRIVATE TOURS AND TEAM BUILDING SCAVENGER HUNTS ARE AVAILABLE. CLICK "TICKETS" FOR DETAILS Tour Itinerary Balboa Park is home to gorgeous gardens, lovely walking paths, and plenty of cultural gems, from theaters to museums featuring the arts, the sciences, and more. You get a full overview of the park and its rich history on this San Diego walking tour that explores how the park has become a symbol of the city. We kick things off at the Founder's Statue on the west side of the park. We think you'll agree San Diegans were visionaries when they set aside 1,400 acres of land for the park all the way back in 1868. Then, we stop at the California Tower, built for the 1915 Panama-California Exposition, which has since become a city landmark. The face of the tower depicts the early history of the city. Next up, we head to Alcazar Gardens, which was inspired by the same-named gardens in Spain. Used for both the 1915 and 1935 expositions, the garden offered fair-goers a respite from the hustle and bustle of the exposition and now provides a picturesque foreground to photos of the California Tower. Snag the perfect Instagram shot while you're here. Following that, we head to the Old Globe Theater which is a Tony Award-winning, world-renowned professional theater group modeled after The Globe Theatre London. From there, we walk through the heart of the Prado, where you get to spy unique building façades and roof supports that most visitors don't notice. We also check out Zoro Gardens, a sunken garden that was once a nudist colony (seriously) during the 1935 California Pacific International Exposition. Today, it's a butterfly garden that contains everything butterflies need for their full life cycle. We then continue past the Bea Evenson fountain, the Natural History Museum, and we pop into the Spanish Village Art Center to observe artists at work in their studios. By now, you should have worked up a thirst so we grab a drink at a local coffee cart before continuing on past one of the largest Morton Bay fig trees in California and the San Diego Junior Theater. But we're not done yet! We then head to the Botanical Building. It's the perfect stop for photographers and flower-lovers alike, and the adjacent lily pond full of koi makes more than just a pretty picture — you learn how the pond was an important part of the war effort during WWI and how it helped injured sailors and soldiers during WWII. Our tour ends at the Plaza de Panama, but before we say goodbye, your guide gives you directions to the nearby Visitor Center (for snacks, maps, souvenirs, and museum passes), suggestions for places to eat, and tips on what else to see and do within the park.

title: San Diego City and Coastline Daily Tour
description: Perfect for first-time visitors and curious locals alike, our San Diego Highlights Daily Tour is a personalized 4-hour journey showcasing the best of America's Finest City. Travel in comfort with your own private driver-guide as you explore San Diego's most iconic neighborhoods and breathtaking coastal views.Begin in the historic Gaslamp Quarter, known for its Victorian-era architecture and vibrant nightlife. Cruise past Petco Park and the modern skyline before heading to Balboa Park, home to over 16 museums, lush gardens, and the world-famous San Diego Zoo.​Cross the scenic Coronado Bridge to explore the charming island of Coronado, where you'll admire the iconic Hotel del Coronado and white-sand beaches. Then, journey north along the coast to the upscale seaside village of La Jolla, where you'll spot sea lions basking on the rocks and stop at the panoramic Mt. Soledad National Veterans Memorial, offering sweeping 360° views of the Pacific Ocean, San Diego skyline, and even Mexico on clear days.

title: Old Town Tales, Tacos and Tequila
description: Step into the birthplace of California with a tour that blends rich history, authentic street food, and tequila! From historic landmarks to handmade tortillas, you'll savor the spirit (and the spirits) of Old Town. Tour Highlights Explore San Diego's most historic neighborhood Learn about the indigenous people who called San Diego home thousands of years before the arrival of European settlers Find out how life changes once the Americans arrive and establish a new government Choose from a vast selection of tequila varieties at an authentic tequileria that boasts a collection of thousands in its museum End your experience enjoying delicious gourmet street tacos Private version of this tour also available. Itinerary As the birthplace of California, Old Town San Diego has seen its fair share of history. Today, it's also widely regarded for its restaurants, margaritas, and souvenir shops. And we'll get to sample a bit of all of these on this 2.5 hour walking tour! We'll start our exploration at the Adobe Chapel which served both as a home and church in the 19th century. Then you'll be treated to handmade tortillas, a world-famous dish that dates back to before Columbus landed in North America. We'll continue the tour and view one of the oldest brick buildings in Southern California, best known for the ghosts who haunt it and see some wonderful examples of Victorian architecture, including San Diego's first synagogue. View the hill where the Spanish settled and learn about the indigenousness people who were here when the Europeans arrived. We'll visit a hundred-year-old Catholic church, followed by a stroll through a landmark cemetery with graves dating back to the 1850s. By now you might be thirsty, so we will take a brief walk to a local tequileria boasting a collection of thousands of bottles. Here you will learn about the many different types of tequilas and be treated to a taste of one of the best tequilas in the world. (This sample is included as part of your tour price, but you may instead choose a margarita, beer or another beverage of your choice). We'll bid adios here, but feel free to linger over another drink, or dig into some great Mexican food. Or, if you want to keep moving, you can continue shopping and exploring this great San Diego neighborhood.

title: Coronado History and Highlights
description: Get off the beaten path and uncover the stories behind Coronado's beautiful homes, historic streets, and seaside splendor. It's a perfect blend of California history, architecture, and coastal vibes. Tour Highlights Discover the hidden history of Coronado — from Victorian elegance to seaside beauty! Marvel at the historic Hotel del Coronado, where celebs and presidents have laid their heads Enjoy a delicious sweet treat at our favorite local eatery Hear stories of the artists, aviators and innovators who made the island their home. PRIVATE TOURS ALSO AVAILABLE. CLICK ON "TICKETS" FOR DETAILS Itinerary A trip to Coronado feels like you're leaving behind the mainland for a dose of island living, but it's not your typical beach town. Part resort, part naval base, part luxury homes for the rich, Coronado is many things to many people. We'll meet at Rotary park on Orange Avenue, the main street in town named after the orange trees planted here in the late 19th century. This main part of town is lined with cafes, shops, and restaurants, almost all locally-owned and operated. We'll work our way down to the public library, the original building donated by John Spreckels. Then we'll get off the main drag and explore some lesser known areas, full of architecture ranging from Gothic to Modern. After a quick sweet treat we'll take a stroll down to Star Park, where the "Wizard of Oz" house is located and then make our way to Coronado beach, one of the best in the country. On a clear day, you can see all the way to Mexico! We stroll by multi-million dollar homes that line Ocean Boulevard and you hear about the people who once lived there, including the residents of the historic Spreckels Mansion. After getting a look at the historic Hotel del Coronado, an 135+ year old icon and American treasure, having hosted celebrities, dignitaries, and US presidents, we'll stroll back to where we started, with a brief stop in one of our favorite shops before we say our goodbyes. Your guide will make suggestions on what else to see and do, and gives you tips on where to eat and shop.

title: Flavors of Little Italy: Food Tour
description: No San Diego Little Italy walking tour would be complete without exploring this culinary hotspot's best places to eat. Known for its variety of restaurants, America's biggest Little Italy has become a food lover's bucket list destination, and we're here to show it off! Join our Little Italy walking tour full of food, fun, and Italian culture. Little Italy is an Italian enclave of great culinary delights in downtown San Diego, and its vibrant atmosphere can be observed during a city tour. San Diego's Italian quarter has grown in popularity for not just Italian fare, but also for the rich array of local restaurant hot-spots that have opened up in the last 10 years, or so. In addition to the traditional pasta and pizza dishes, Little Italy offers up top chef cuisine, vegan breakfasts, juicy burgers, and lounges for dancing and fine cocktails! Consider every day a Little Italy food fest! The streets of Little Italy are lined with Italian American staples, making the Little Italy Tour a must-try experience among travelers visiting San Diego. The possibilities to whet your palate are endless. After all, what better way to savor the delicious food paired with sangria, beer, or wine tasting. Our Little Italy tours will have you savoring iconic bites while you get an understanding of the history and current vibe of the neighborhood with one of our professional and personable tour guides. A walking tour of the exciting history of Little Italy Taste a combination of Italian flavors and unique bites from 4 restaurants that represent the area Learn about the early fishing industry of San Diego Meet some of the local Italians and Italian immigrants who've been a part of the revitalization of the area Wine, sangria or local beer tasters Days/Times Available*: Monday through Thursday at 4:30pm Rate (Age 13+): $88 per Person Duration: 2.5 Hours Minimum Guests: 4 / Maximum Guests: 15

title: ALL-NEW Shark Encounter
description: ALL-NEW Shark Encounter to Open May 22, 2026 Come face-to-fin with some of the ocean's most fascinating predators in a newly refreshed exhibit: Encounter mesmerizing new shark species alongside a vibrant array of marine life—including additional sharks and colorful fish—as the expanded exhibit transforms into a dynamic underwater adventure. Experience the ocean like never before in a breathtakingly enhanced space that pulls guests into an immersive realm—celebrating the beauty and power of sharks while challenging long-held myths about this extraordinary species. The all-new Shark Encounter steps beyond traditional fear-based portrayals to educate guests about the incredible diversity of sharks—over 400 species worldwide—highlighting their varied sizes, shapes, and fascinating adaptations that contribute to healthy oceans. This is more than a refresh—it's a commitment: The reimagined experience continues to showcase the stunning array of sharks, with 11 different species including sand tiger sharks, blacktip reef sharks, and the endangered Australian leopard shark—part of a Species Survival Plan through AZA-accredited facilities. Almost all the species at Shark Encounter are listed as at least vulnerable or near threatened, reinforcing SeaWorld's commitment to education and species protection. Stunning visual upgrades and immersive storytelling: State-of-the-art multimedia brings the new experience to life, fusing striking visuals with education-driven storytelling to deepen the connection between humans and one of the ocean's most iconic predators. An overhead video ceiling in the entry hallway features sharks swimming above, guiding guests toward the underwater tunnel. In the finale, a multi-screen installation surrounds you with rare live footage of shark diversity in a mesmerizing 8-minute presentation. Exclusive VIP Shark Tour: As part of the all-new experience, adventurers will have the chance to learn up-close how our zoological specialists feed and care for the sharks. The tour includes access to a brand-new walkway above the shark habitat, offering a dramatic, elevated view of these fascinating animals from a unique perspective. This unforgettable experience promotes deeper understanding, personal connection, and marine life conservation. Purchase details coming soon!

title: Petco Park Tours
description: Experience Petco Park like never before with a behind-the-scenes ballpark tour! Guests will have the opportunity to explore exclusive areas of the stadium, learn about the history of the San Diego Padres, and take in one of baseball's most iconic venues from a unique perspective. Tours are available on select dates throughout the season; however, tours do not operate on certain event days, holidays, and select game or stadium blackout dates. On concert days, tour times may be adjusted to accommodate event operations. Tours are currently not available on the following dates. This list is subject to change. 2026: March 7 (San Diego Science Expo) March 21-22 (Savannah Bananas) April 30 (Full Ballpark Event) May 13-16 (Full Ballpark Event) June 11-14 (Field of Dreamz Festival) June 16-19 (Fuerza Rediga) July 19-22 (Ed Sheeran Concert) August 13-16 (Rufus Du Sol) August 15-18 (Noah Kahan) August 28-31 (My Chemical Romance Concert) September 10-13 (Pierce the Veil) On Gallagher Square concerts days, we adjust our tour schedule as follows: Weekday: The 2:00 PM tour will not be available Weekends: The 1:00 PM and 2:00 PM tours will not be available Please refer to the Petco Park event schedule to view upcoming concert dates. For the most up-to-date tour schedule, blackout dates, and adjusted concert tour times, please visit the Padres Tours page.

title: Fight Inflation on This Elevated Wine Tasting
description: This event offers a VIP wine tasting experience at a low price, including door-to-door transportation, ample wine pours, a wine tour, and a winery meal. It features local San Diego County wineries and provides a fully guided adventure with entertaining commentary on local history and viticulture. The tours run from Wednesday through Sunday for groups ranging from 2 to 100 guests, welcoming single guests as well.

title: San Diego Beer, Wine, and Spirits
description: Head to the original California wine country for a memorable tasting experience. Explore the natural beauty of the vineyards while savoring the unique flavors of the region's world-class wines.

title: Klara Chavarria Art Gallery
description: The Klara Chavarria Art Gallery showcases the captivating work of artist Klara Chavarría, featuring a vibrant collection of internationally acclaimed abstract and figurative expressionism. The gallery offers a profound exploration of human emotions and existence through contemporary fine art. The event recurs monthly on the first Friday through January 1, 2027, providing an ongoing opportunity to experience Klara's unique artistic vision.

title: Old Town Trolley’s Holiday Sights and Festive Nights Tour
description: Old Town Trolley’s Holiday Sights and Festive Nights Tour in San Diego invites families and friends to experience the city’s festive spirit from the comfort of a decorated trolley. Guests are treated to cheerful holiday music, candy canes, and interactive sing-alongs with lyric books, making the ride lively and engaging. Knowledgeable guides share fun stories and lead playful games, creating a warm and memorable holiday outing.

title: A Very Furry Christmas
description: A Very Furry Christmas at Sesame Place San Diego transforms the park into a cheerful holiday wonderland, inviting families to celebrate the season with beloved Sesame Street characters. Guests can enjoy festive shows, holiday-themed attractions, and interactive experiences designed for all ages. The event features dazzling decorations and plenty of opportunities for memorable photos with favorite furry friends. It’s a joyful way to create lasting holiday memories in a playful, welcoming setting.

title: Shelter Island Farmers Market
description: Shelter Island Friday Farmers Market is a weekly waterfront market held every Friday at the Island Palms Hotel and Marina. The market features local artisans, makers, and small businesses offering fresh sourdough, honey, desserts, clothing, skincare, resin art, hats, bags, hot tea, and more. Visitors can enjoy the market's laid-back atmosphere with beautiful Marina views and island vibes while supporting local vendors.

title: LEGO® Galaxy NOW OPEN at LEGOLAND® California
description: LEGO® Galaxy is an ALL-NEW space-themed land at LEGOLAND® California where families can launch into a world of imagination, creativity, and adventure inspired by decades of LEGO space stories. Guests can explore an immersive environment filled with impressive brick built space models, interactive experiences, and character meet and greets. The land brings together classic and modern LEGO space themes, inviting explorers of all ages to discover distant worlds, train like astronauts, and see LEGO creativity taken to a whole new universe. At the heart of LEGO Galaxy is a collection of adventures designed for the whole family, including Galacticoaster, an indoor family coaster, along with additional space inspired rides and a dedicated toddler play zone. Guests can build and test LEGO rockets, enjoy hands on play areas, and refuel with galactic themed food and treats before stopping by Orbital Outpost to pick up space ready souvenirs. With rides, dining, shopping, and playful storytelling all in one place, LEGO Galaxy offers an out of this world experience for young space explorers and their families. Now Open since March 6th, 2026

title: Midday Sail
description: This is what sailing in San Diego is all about! Hop aboard one of our ticketed midday sails or book the whole boat just for you and your crew! If you've got four or more passengers, a private sail is definitely the way to go, and you'll have the whole boat to yourself! Ticketed vs. Private: Ticketed sails are great for solo adventurers, couples, and groups of 4 or less. Private sails are highly recommended for families and groups of 4+. Enjoy 3 hours of sailing during the best time of the day! More info: Peak Sailing Conditions: The wind's up, the boat's moving, and you're getting the real sailing experience. Wildlife in Action: Midday is prime time for spotting dolphins, sea lions, and maybe even a whale. San Diego at Its Best: Clear skies, wide-open water, and views you won't get shoreside. Plenty of Time for More: A solid adventure without killing the rest of your day. Captains Who Know Their Stuff: USCG-certified, super experienced, and actually fun to hang out with. Cold Drinks Included: Beer, hard seltzer, soft drinks, and snacks — all on the house.

title: Sunset Sail
description: Nothing beats a San Diego sunset, except for a San Diego Sunset Cruise. Experience the best views of golden hour and downtown San Diego from the deck of a sailboat on the San Diego Bay. A Sunset Cruise Worth Slowing Down For: No traffic or noisy bars, and nothing between you and the horizon. Perfect For Date Night (or Any Night): Anniversaries, birthdays, or just an excuse to cuddle up with your boo. Small Group Sailing Experience: There's no such thing as a bad view from the deck of our premium sailboats. Captains Who Know Their Stuff: USCG-certified, super experienced, and actually fun to hang out with. Start Your Evening with a Toast: We've got beer, hard seltzer, soft drinks, and snacks waiting on board for you. Book a Ticketed Sunset Sail or Private Sunset Sail. Ticketed charters are capped at 6 passengers max; private boats can be booked for groups up to 12 passengers.

title: Large Group Sails
description: This is how locals sail San Diego. This vessel is ideal for larger groups, featuring a spacious deck for relaxing or socializing, a full galley below deck, two staterooms, and two bathrooms on board for your comfort and convenience. This sailboat can comfortably accommodate groups up to 22 passengers Includes drinks (waters and sodas), snacks, and experienced captain + crew on board Optional: Upgrade your sail with a bar package Bar Package: The bar package features a selection of lagers, hard seltzers, and a variety of wines for you to enjoy, including white wine and rosé. Customize your charter: you choose your departure time and if you would like to sail for 2,3 or 4 hours. It's your private sailboat! Perfect for large gatherings, birthdays, family reunions, small weddings, and corporate events.

title: Painted Prayers: The Japanese Folk Art Tradition of Ema
description: The Painted Prayers exhibition highlights the Japanese folk painting tradition of ema, which are house-shaped wooden votive plaques used as prayers in Shinto shrines and Buddhist temples. The exhibition presents a variety of ema, ranging from the 18th century to the present day, showcasing their colorful imagery and the prayers associated with them. Curated by Japanese art historian Meher McArthur, this is the first museum exhibition in the United States to display such an extensive collection of ema.

title: 10% Off Mon-Thurs Intimate Whale Watching Tours
description: Experience the thrill of being up close to nature on a small, fast, rigid inflatable boat! We glide through the ocean on a military decommissioned boat on a wildlife safari to see whales, dolphins, sea-lions, and so much more! Our boats are low profile which allows us to get arms reach away from wildlife. On our educational, guided tours every passenger gets to enjoy 360-degree, front row views and one-on-one time to talk to the captain. With no crowds, no hassle, and a personalized tour, there is no question why Adventure Whale Watching has been given 5-star ratings by almost every explorer! With small group sizes we are able to personalize every tour to give each of our guests their own unique and memorable experience! Join us for the adventure of a lifetime! Make your reservation today!Adventurewhalewatchingsandiego.com(619) 808-2822 Who we are: At Adventure Whale Watching we specialize in fast-moving, fun boat tours on Navy Seal Rigid Inflatable Boats (R.I.B.s). We are one of the only year-round Whale & Dolphin Watching companies in San Diego! Our boats set us apart because they are low profile which creates stability so it eliminates the aggressive rocking that you experience on the bigger, slow-moving boats. Our boats are also faster than typical whale watching tours which means that we are able to cover more area allowing us to see more and get closer to the wildlife. We have a small number of passengers per boat which will ensure that the boat is never crowded and that each of the guests gets a personalized experience with 360 degree views from every seat! Educational: Our captains are also naturalists and marine biologists who will educate you about the wildlife, the boats, and give you some history about San Diego! We have NOAA Marine Mammal Identification Guides we use to help passengers learn about and identify the potential species we can see on the tour. We also have tide charts and Boating Safety Information. We also talk about the Marine Mammal Protection Act, Entanglements, and Environmental Conservation. We may even pick up some balloons or water bottles if we find garbage in the ocean to help our marine friends!Safety: Our tours are guided by United States Coast Guard certified captains and naturalists aboard military decommissioned Rigid Inflatable Boats (R.I.B.s). These low-profile boats are some of the safest boats on the water! Additionally, we offer each passenger a life vest and the choice of a snow suit or a military survival suit to keep them warm and dry while on the boat. Join Us! Winter excursions, between December 15th & March 31st, are offered daily at 8:30am, 11:30am, and 2:30pm. Summer excursions, between April 1st & October 14th, are offered daily at 8:30, 12:00pm, & 3:30pm. Fall excursions, between October 15th & December 14th, are offered daily at 8:30 & 12:00pm. Pricing: The price for 2.5 - 3 hour Public Whale Watching tours are $129 plus tax per guest. Private Whale Watching tours are offered for $775 plus tax for up to 6 passengers. Use code WEEKDAY10 for 10% Monday - Thursday tours! Please call to accommodate groups larger than 6. Reservations are required.

title: Skate Craft
description: Skate Craft celebrates skateboarding's do-it-yourself values, tracing its evolution from handmade decks and ramps to contemporary handcrafted art. The exhibition highlights the creativity, self-expression, and craftsmanship involved in skateboard design, safety gear, and skate shoes. It presents skateboarding as a form of living folk art that combines play, design, and sustainability.

title: Alma Unleashed: A Canine Social
description: Unwind with your pup at Alma Unleashed: A Canine Social—a daily happy hour at the Lobby Bar & Patio. Bring your dog and enjoy 20% off select food and beverages from 4:00-6:00 PM, where great drinks and good dogs go hand in hand. Must be 21+ to consume alcoholic beverages. Must bring dog to redeem discount. Not valid with any other offer or discount. Dogs must be leashed and supervised at all times. Management reserves all rights.

title: Take Me to the Water: Histories of the Black Pacific Exhibit
description: Opening Saturday, May 23 • Memorial Day Weekend 2026Take Me to the Water: Histories of the Black Pacific opens at the Maritime Museum of San Diego and is included with general admission. Take Me to the Water: Histories of the Black Pacific explores the deep and often overlooked relationships between Black communities and the Pacific Ocean. Spanning centuries of maritime movement, labor, exploration, and cultural exchange, the exhibit highlights the lives and contributions of Black mariners whose stories helped shape the Pacific world. Visitors are invited to discover the experiences of sailors, whalers, fishers, waterfront workers, explorers, soldiers, surfers, swimmers, and scholars whose connections to the ocean expanded far beyond traditional narratives of maritime history. Through immersive storytelling, historical interpretation, and contemporary scholarship, the exhibit offers a broader and more inclusive understanding of the Pacific—revealing how identity, labor, migration, resilience, and culture moved across oceans and generations. Developed in partnership with UC San Diego curator Dr. Caroline Collins, Take Me to the Water: Histories of the Black Pacific brings together research, storytelling, and maritime history to explore Black relationships with the Pacific Ocean and the broader maritime world. The exhibit invites visitors to reconsider familiar narratives of exploration, labor, and seafaring through stories that have too often remained outside traditional maritime histories.

title: Private Chauffeured Vineyards Adventure
description: Private Chauffeured Vineyards Adventure includes many attractions in ONE! The finest local wines; delicious farm-to-table food; hidden gems; sightseeing; history; culture; fully guided tour for the all-inclusive price of only $234 per person (includes all fees and gratuities). PERFECT for teambuilding, corporate groups. 3 to 6 hour food and wine tasting experiences available. Timing and offering can be fully customized. • Enjoy the local (close-by) San Diego County wineries, not Temecula (San Diego is the original California Wine Country) • With thousands of 5-STAR reviews, you'll receive top-notch treatment! • Serving tens of thousands of happy guests since 2010. Highlights of the Chauffeured Vineyards Adventure include: - VIP guided adventure to stunning and unique local winery estates.- Ample pours of award-winning local wines.- A delicious appetizer platte AND a full meal!- Fun edutainment. Entertaining commentary on local history, and viticulture.- Door-to-door transportation (from most San Diego locations).- Wine tasting appreciation presentation, and guided winery tour.
